channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Clutch’s First Full Trailer Shown At Summer Games Fest
item.content:encoded:
Clutch is the debut title from Maverick Games. The studio comprises former Forza Horizon developers. The game was officially announced earlier in the week, but at Summer Games Fest 2026, the First full trailer was shown off.

This isn’t your normal racing simulation. This game is described as a cinematic, open-world, action-driving game. The story follows Theo, voiced by Tosin Cole, on a thrilling adventure that sees him risk his life to compete in the Riviera 1000 by day and in the underground street-racing scene at night.

More on Clutch

Mike Brown, the game’s creative director, was recently interviewed by IGN, where he shared more details about Clutch and his vision for it.

“There was that spell where Need for Speed – from Need for Speed Underground, Underground 2, and Most Wanted – was the number one best-selling game in the world, like, three years running. There was that period where Top Gear was the most-watched TV show and, in film, even though it’s not the biggest film franchise in the world, the Fast & Furious series is one of them.

“It’s made more money than Harry Potter; it’s made more money than Batman. And that is the level of ambition for Clutch, to set the tone for car culture across the next decade, and be that tentpole IP that drives culture forward.”

Players can check out the full synopsis for the game below:

Set in the French Riviera – follow the story (written by Skins co-creator, Jamie Brittain) of Theo (Tosin Cole) and Cass Martial (Little Simz) through a thrilling adventure that sees them compete in the prestigous Riviera 1000 (R1K) by day, and the underground street-racing scene of the Midnight Collective by night.

After Theo and Cass’ troubled upbringing in the UK, they are adopted by former R1K superstar Ludo Martial (Gregory Montel) before earning their way to a pair of seats in the R1K.

Following a spate of tragedies befalling the sport, R1K head George McBride (Peter Serafinowicz) shocks the racing world by introducing disruptive technology to avoid any more such disasters.

However, this berths a divide in the integrity of the sport, calling Theo to the allure of the Midnight Collective. Trouble follows and after recruiting the services of fixer, Emery Whitman (Jane Perry), the underbelly of the city and R1K is blown wide open.

Amongst the professional racing of the R1K and the style of the Midnight Collective, there are thousands of miles of diverse roads and terrain to explore, from breathtaking coastal highways to vibrant city streets.

Blockbuster action, deep vehicle customisation, pursuits, getaways, innovative vehicle tech, and so much more – it’s all in CLUTCH.

Insider Gaming will continue to share more news about the future of Clutch whenever more details get shared. Check out everything announced during Summer Games Fest here. Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Wolf Among Us Remastered Release Window Revealed
item.content:encoded:
During the latest Summer Game Fest event, Telltale Games announced that The Wolf Among Us Remastered will be released in Holiday 2026.

The announcement was unexpected, especially since many players believed that The Wolf Among Us 2 would be the only reveal from Telltale Games. However, the team had the players in mind, introducing a new game that fans can enjoy while they wait for the next main installment.

The Wolf Among Us Remastered Releases Holiday 2026

No specific features or details were revealed for The Wolf Among Us Remastered, apart from its release window. We only received a brief glimpse of the characters, but it didn’t provide much information. However, similar to other remasters, players can expect enhanced visuals with improved fidelity and performance. Since the original game was released in 2023, there is likely substantial potential for improvements on current-generation consoles and systems.

The Wolf Among Us comes from the makers of The Walking Dead, where players can experience a brand-new, violent, and mature thriller based on the award-winning Fables comic books. You embody the role of Bigby Wolf, who realizes that a brutal, bloody murder is just the start of things that players could experience with every decision they make, as every choice has its consequences. The original title saw the whole season spread across 5 episodes.

In other news, the Ascendance DLC for Monster Hunter World 3 has been unveiled at Summer Game Fest. Additionally, a release window for Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 has been revealed at today’s Summer Game Fest. What are your thoughts on The Wolf Among Us Remastered releasing in Holiday 2026? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Stellar Blade Blood Rain being announced at Summer Game Fest. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
